summary: Iteration 18 — validated (A) the sitemap-rendering bug fix and (B) the new Bing Shopping feed routes. New backend regression suite /app/backend/tests/test_iter18_bing_feed_sitemap.py (36 tests). Result: 35/36 pass on first run; the 1 failure was an ingress-cache-header difference (origin returns 'public, max-age=3600' but the kubernetes ingress at the preview URL overrides Cache-Control to 'no-store, no-cache, must-revalidate'). Fixed the test to assert headers against the origin (localhost:3000) as the request explicitly says 'Origin cache headers'. Final run: 36/36 PASS for iter-18 suite. Full /app/backend/tests/ run: 131 passed / 4 skipped / 1 failed (the single failure is the SAME pre-existing test-helper bug from iter-17 — test_product_ads_seo.py::test_title_long_format_short_name — NOT a real product regression). Frontend Playwright run on /sitemap.xml confirmed the XSLT transforms the raw XML into a styled HTML table: title='XML Sitemap — Maventech Software', 91 tbody rows, all 5 columns present (#, URL, Last Modified, Frequency, Priority), 91 priority pills (56 prio-high, 16 prio-med, 19 prio-low), every URL is <a target='_blank'>. The admin AI-Blogger 'View Sitemap' button [data-testid='view-sitemap-btn'] has the correct href ending '/sitemap.xml'.

minor.issue: Same test-helper edge case documented in iteration_17.json. windows-11-pro renders <title>='Buy Windows 11 Pro | Lifetime License Key | $79.99' (50 chars, 3-pipe format intact, format-compliant) but the helper _title_prefix() over-strips the price segment after seo_clamp_title already dropped the brand suffix. Product output is correct; only the test helper needs a patch. NOT introduced by iter-18 work.

sitemap_rendering_bug_fix.raw_xml_status: 200, application/xml; starts with <?xml-stylesheet type='text/xsl' href='/assets/sitemap.xsl'?> right after the XML decl
sitemap_rendering_bug_fix.xsl_asset_status: /assets/sitemap.xsl returns 200 application/xml, contains <xsl:stylesheet>, <xsl:template match='/'>, 'XML Sitemap' header, and prio-high/prio-med/prio-low CSS classes
sitemap_rendering_bug_fix.browser_rendering: Title='XML Sitemap — Maventech Software', 91 styled table rows, 5 columns (#, URL, Last Modified, Frequency, Priority), 91 priority pills (56 prio-high / 16 prio-med / 19 prio-low), every URL link has target='_blank' rel='noopener'
sitemap_rendering_bug_fix.admin_button: [data-testid='view-sitemap-btn'] present on /admin.php?tab=ai-blogger with href ending /sitemap.xml (button is inside a collapsed accordion at first load — that's a benign UX detail, the data-testid + href are correct)

bing_shopping_feed.routes_200: all 4 new Bing routes (/feed/bing-shopping.xml, /feeds/bing-shopping.xml, /bing-shopping-feed.xml, /microsoft-merchant-feed.xml) return HTTP 200 application/xml; all 5 existing Google routes (/merchant-feed.xml, /feed/google-products.xml, /feeds/google-products.xml, /google-merchant-feed.xml, /google-shopping-feed.xml) also still return 200
bing_shopping_feed.bing_native_rss_aliases: every Bing <item> contains <title>, <link>, <description>, <guid isPermaLink='true'>, <pubDate> ALONGSIDE the g:* tags (verified across all 37 items)
bing_shopping_feed.google_no_aliases: every Google <item> has NO default-namespace title/link/description/guid/pubDate — only g:* tags
bing_shopping_feed.channel_title_diff: Bing channel <title> ends with 'Bing Shopping Feed'; Google channel <title> ends with 'Software Product Feed'
bing_shopping_feed.atom_self_link: for each of the 4 Bing routes, <atom:link rel='self' href='...'> ends with the exact request path that was hit

validated_features.robots_txt: lists three required Sitemap directives ending in /merchant-feed.xml, /feed/google-products.xml, /feed/bing-shopping.xml (plus /sitemap.xml, /llms.txt, /agents.json)

regression.item_count_37: both Bing and Google feeds emit exactly 37 <item> blocks
regression.g_product_highlight_148: both feeds emit exactly 148 <g:product_highlight> (4 per item × 37)
regression.origin_cache_headers: Cache-Control: public, max-age=3600; no Set-Cookie/Pragma/Expires on origin (localhost:3000). NOTE: the kubernetes ingress on the public preview host overrides Cache-Control to 'no-store, no-cache, must-revalidate' — that is an ingress/CDN layer behavior, NOT a code regression. If production needs cacheable feeds end-to-end, the ingress rule for /feed/*.xml / *.xml endpoints should be reviewed.
regression.product_jsonld_iter17: Product JSON-LD on /product.php?slug=windows-11-pro still has offers.shippingDetails as array of 6 entries and offers.hasMerchantReturnPolicy with applicableCountry array

action_items: OPTIONAL — Patch test_product_ads_seo.py::_title_prefix helper (pre-existing from iter-17, product output is compliant).

OPTIONAL — Investigate why the kubernetes ingress at show-live-7.preview.emergentagent.com overrides Cache-Control on /feed/*.xml from 'public, max-age=3600' to 'no-store, no-cache, must-revalidate'. The origin is correct; this is an ingress-layer concern. In production behind a CDN/proxy this may matter for crawler cache efficiency.
critical_code_review_comments: /app/php-version/assets/sitemap.xsl — clean, presentation-only stylesheet. Correctly declares output method='html', exposes light + dark color schemes, and uses xsl:choose to color-code priority pills. The xsl:value-of for $p (numeric priority) renders as text content of the pill, which is the right call.

/app/php-version/sitemap-xml.php — placing the <?xml-stylesheet?> PI immediately after the XML decl is the correct order; browsers won't apply XSLT if the PI appears after <urlset>. Search engines (Google/Bing) skip PIs they don't understand so this is safe.

/app/php-version/router.php — all 9 alias routes (5 Google + 4 Bing) cleanly route to merchant-feed.php. The Bing routes correctly drive $isBingMode from the request path (so a single feed generator handles both flavors).

/app/php-version/merchant-feed.php — per-item RSS-native aliases (<title>, <link>, <description>, <guid isPermaLink='true'>, <pubDate>) emitted only inside the Bing branch. Microsoft Merchant Center accepts either set; emitting both makes the feed portable. Google routes stay slim — correct.

/app/php-version/robots-txt.php — three Sitemap directives now point to /merchant-feed.xml, /feed/google-products.xml, /feed/bing-shopping.xml. Note: robots.txt also lists /sitemap.xml, /llms.txt, /agents.json as Sitemap directives — that exceeds the 'THREE' in the request but is additive and harmless (search engines tolerate multiple Sitemap lines).

MINOR — The 'View Sitemap' button [data-testid='view-sitemap-btn'] in /admin.php?tab=ai-blogger is inside a collapsed accordion section on first load (not 'visible' to Playwright until expanded). data-testid + href are 100% correct so click flow works; just a UX note if user testing expects it above-the-fold.

success_rate.backend: 100% on iter-18 scope (36/36). 131/132 across full /app/backend/tests/ — single failure is the pre-existing test-helper bug from iter-17, unrelated to iter-18.
success_rate.frontend: 100% on iter-18 scope (XSLT-rendered sitemap loads with all assertions met; admin button data-testid + href verified).

context_for_next_testing_agent: Iter-18 adds /app/backend/tests/test_iter18_bing_feed_sitemap.py as the primary regression for: (a) sitemap.xml XSLT rendering + /assets/sitemap.xsl reachability, (b) all 9 merchant-feed routes (4 Bing + 5 Google), (c) per-item native RSS aliases in Bing mode only, (d) channel <title> differentiation, (e) atom:link self-href echo, (f) robots.txt Sitemap directives. Cache-header assertion runs against http://localhost:3000 (origin), NOT the external preview URL — the kubernetes ingress overrides Cache-Control. Full suite: pytest /app/backend/tests/ -v
rca of the issue: Sitemap rendering bug (user's screenshot showed wall-of-XML): root cause was the absence of an XSLT stylesheet processing instruction in /sitemap.xml; modern browsers won't apply a stylesheet that isn't referenced from the document. Fix: added <?xml-stylesheet type='text/xsl' href='/assets/sitemap.xsl'?> right after the XML declaration in /app/php-version/sitemap-xml.php, and created /app/php-version/assets/sitemap.xsl (HTML output method with table layout, dark-mode aware, priority pills). Search engines ignore the PI so SEO is unaffected. Verified end-to-end via Playwright: title 'XML Sitemap — Maventech Software', 91 styled rows, 5 columns, 91 colored priority pills.
